question: Conversation 14f3c888 was only a request to reformat a video transcript verbatim. Should it produce a project at all?
claude_interpretation: No project; non-knowledge one-off. The app-building content belongs to the third-party transcript being formatted.
codex_interpretation: Emitted a project (medium confidence, flagged for review) titled by the user's opening line.
practical_consequence: Keeping it invents a 'transcript formatting' project and may attribute a third party's AI-digest app to the user.
recommended_default: Adopt Claude: do not store as a project.

question: Is conversation 2838a734 a real project/assignment (Claude) or a false project (the selection label)?
claude_interpretation: A real graded academic ASSIGNMENT (pesticide risk assessment) plus an adopted Claude-in-Excel workflow.
codex_interpretation: A high-confidence project (selection had labelled it false_project).
practical_consequence: Determines whether a real coursework deliverable is captured and at what granularity (assignment vs generic project).
recommended_default: Store as a real work entity typed 'assignment'.

question: Is conversation 94f53cfc one project (Codex) or five distinct activities (Claude)?
claude_interpretation: Five separate work entities: lecture-study initiative, OS lab, Amazon SDE resume, YouTube policy prompt, linear-algebra exam prep.
codex_interpretation: One project ('I want to make me chat gpt promt').
practical_consequence: Over-merging fuses unrelated coursework/career activities into one fictitious project; over-splitting could fragment a single study habit.
recommended_default: Split into multiple work entities (Claude); optionally keep a lightweight 'study helper' umbrella.

question: Are the 2024 study-helper user and the 2026 roadmap/Unity/film user the same person, and should context be linked across conversations?
claude_interpretation: Very likely the same CS student (profile + name 'Yohannes' match); linked via associated_with, flagged.
codex_interpretation: No cross-conversation entity linking; no context entities emitted at all.
practical_consequence: Cross-conversation identity resolution affects whether the future DB builds one coherent user profile or many fragments.
recommended_default: Treat as same user; confirm before merging profiles.

question: Did the user actually accept the assistant's recommendation to skip the ExamPro course?
claude_interpretation: No explicit acceptance preserved; recorded as assistant-origin proposed direction with implicit continuation.
practical_consequence: Storing it as a user decision would assert a choice the user never explicitly made.

question: Does the user actually have an Accenture internship / Tech-Analyst role (244ba1ae)?
claude_interpretation: Unconfirmed; the affiliation is an assistant inference the user never validates. Stored low-confidence, flagged.
codex_interpretation: Not represented (conversation kept as one-off only).
practical_consequence: Storing it as fact would put an unverified employer in the user's profile.
recommended_default: Do not store as a confirmed employer; keep flagged or drop.

question: Which projects are CURRENT vs historical? (roadmap active; Unity & film completed spring 2026; study-helper 2024 historical)
claude_interpretation: Roadmap=active/current; Fleet Command & Tsotsi=completed/historical; 2024 study activities=historical.
codex_interpretation: All six projects marked temporal_status=current (including the 2024 study chat and submitted spring-2026 projects).
practical_consequence: Wrong currency misleads any 'what is the user working on now' query.

question: Are the professor's GDD/SDD a project source/reference or an active artifact the user produced?
claude_interpretation: Source/reference material received from the professor (not user-authored artifact).
codex_interpretation: Not represented (no attachments surfaced; bundle attachment list empty).
practical_consequence: Determines provenance of the requirements docs and whether the missing attachments must be re-ingested.
recommended_default: Treat as source; re-ingest attachments (attachment_heavy bundle surfaced zero).
